title: A survey of software refactoring
authors: Tom Mens; Tom Tourwe
relevance_to_p1: Establishes the breadth of refactoring as a maintenance activity.
core_claim: Refactoring research spans activities, tooling, and formalisms across software artifacts.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to ground the claim that structural code change is routine and important.

title: Refactoring in the Wild: A Study of Refactoring Decisions in Open Source Projects
authors: Miryung Kim; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Provides empirical evidence that real projects perform refactorings in practice.
core_claim: Refactoring decisions in open-source projects are frequent, contextual, and maintenance-driven.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to justify studying evolution rather than toy source transformations alone.
notes: No local PDF in current corpus.

title: A Large-Scale Study on the Usage of Refactoring Operations
authors: Nikos Tsantalis; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Strengthens the claim that refactoring operations are common and diverse.
core_claim: Large-scale mining reveals broad use of refactoring operations in practice.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to support the refactoring catalog and its practical relevance.
notes: No local PDF in current corpus.

title: Studying the co-evolution of production and test code in open source and industrial developer test processes through repository mining
authors: Andy Zaidman; Bart Van Rompaey; Arie van Deursen; Serge Demeyer
relevance_to_p1: Supports the maintenance framing behind P1.
core_claim: Software artifacts co-evolve over time and should be studied longitudinally rather than in isolation.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to motivate revision-history and maintenance-oriented evaluation thinking.

title: Automatic Software Repair: A Survey
authors: Luca Gazzola; Daniela Micucci; Leonardo Mariani
relevance_to_p1: Frames repair as a recognized SE problem but for code defects, not proof maintenance.
core_claim: APR pipelines generate and validate candidate fixes against an oracle.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as contrast: P1 studies proof brittleness and baseline recovery, not defect repair itself.
notes: APR analogy should be kept bounded.

title: TacTok: semantics-aware proof synthesis
authors: Emily First; Yuriy Brun; Arjun Guha
venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
relevance_to_p1: Relevant as a proof-automation baseline family, not as the target problem.
core_claim: Proof scripts share enough structure to enable learned synthesis from proof corpora.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to position P1 baselines against proof synthesis rather than maintenance-specific reuse.

title: Passport: Improving Automated Formal Verification Using Identifiers
authors: Alex Sanchez-Stern; Emily First; Timothy Zhou; Zhanna Kaufman; Yuriy Brun; Talia Ringer
venue: ACM Transactions on Programming Languages and Systems
relevance_to_p1: Useful for positioning P1 relative to learning-based proof automation.
core_claim: Identifier-aware representations improve automated proof synthesis in proof corpora.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as contrast: automation over corpora exists, but P1 studies breakage under evolution.

title: CodeBERT: A Pre-Trained Model for Programming and Natural Languages
authors: Zhangyin Feng; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Context for LLM/code-model baselines used in the broader repair literature.
core_claim: Pretrained models can learn joint natural-language and code representations.
how_p1_uses_it: Use only to frame the LLM baseline family at a high level.
notes: Do not overclaim direct verification relevance.

title: GraphCodeBERT: Pre-training Code Representations with Data Flow
authors: Daya Guo; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Complements CodeBERT as a structured code-representation baseline family.
core_claim: Data-flow-aware pretraining improves code representations.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as part of the LLM/code-repair background, not as direct P1 evidence.

title: InCoder: A Generative Model for Code Infilling and Synthesis
authors: Daniel Fried; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Useful for contextualizing generative repair-style baselines.
core_claim: Decoder-style code infilling models can generate code and edits from context.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to explain the style of the LLM-only baseline family.

title: Automated Program Repair with Pretrained Language Models
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Bridges APR and pretrained language models, which is directly relevant to P1's B3 framing.
core_claim: PLMs can be used as repair engines under generate-and-validate workflows.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as the most direct code-repair contrast for LLM-only repair.

title: Towards Formal Proof Script Refactoring
authors: Iain Whiteside; David Aspinall; Lucas Dixon; Gudmund Grov
relevance_to_p1: Directly relevant to proof refactoring and maintenance.
core_claim: Formal proof-script refactorings can be defined and reasoned about as semantics-preserving transformations.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to show that proof maintenance has been studied in interactive settings, but not in P1's empirical benchmark form.
notes: Kept despite download failure because it is conceptually central.

title: Refactoring Proofs
authors: Iain Johnston Whiteside
relevance_to_p1: Expands the proof-refactoring line into a full maintenance-oriented framework.
core_claim: Proof refactoring can be formalized with a dedicated language and a large catalog of semantics-preserving proof transformations.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as depth behind the proof-maintenance literature, especially for the claim that proof artifacts themselves can be systematically refactored.
notes: Retained from repository metadata and search snippets.

title: Refactoring Proofs with Tactician
authors: Mark Adams
relevance_to_p1: Provides tool support evidence for proof refactoring in interactive theorem proving.
core_claim: Tactic proof scripts can be compacted and restructured to improve maintainability and reuse.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as a concrete tool-oriented example showing proof refactoring has operational support in ITP settings.
notes: Kept via official project page snippet.

title: Theorem Reuse by Proof Term Transformation
authors: Einar Broch Johnsen; Olaf Owe; Ingrid Chieh Yu
relevance_to_p1: Supports the broader theme of reusing proof artifacts across related results.
core_claim: Proof terms can be transformed to generalize and reuse existing theorems.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as adjacent reuse work: existing proof artifacts can sometimes be repurposed rather than rebuilt.
notes: Useful but secondary in the final section.

title: Mechanised Verification Patterns for Dafny
authors: Gudmund Grov; Yuhui Lin; Vytautas Tumas
relevance_to_p1: Important Dafny-specific evidence that users need reusable proof guidance patterns.
core_claim: Dafny verification often requires recurring patterns and user guidance beyond the surface program text.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to support the claim that Dafny proofs have recognizable maintenance and guidance patterns, even before refactoring is studied directly.
notes: Search snippets provide title, venue, authors, and abstract fragment.

title: Dafny: An Automatic Program Verifier for Functional Correctness
authors: K. Rustan M. Leino
relevance_to_p1: Foundational source for positioning P1 in the Dafny ecosystem.
core_claim: Dafny is an auto-active verifier combining program text, specs, and automated proof obligations.
how_p1_uses_it: Use sparingly to anchor the verifier setting and the auto-active verification model.
notes: Metadata taken from mirrored PDF/search results.

title: Dafny Meets the Verification Benchmarks Challenge
authors: K. Rustan M. Leino
relevance_to_p1: Useful historical benchmark context for Dafny.
core_claim: Dafny can serve as a vehicle for benchmark-style verification case studies.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to show that benchmarks in Dafny exist, but not yet P1's refactoring-maintenance benchmark.
notes: Good historical bridge to newer benchmark work.

title: Testing Dafny (Experience Paper)
authors: Ahmed Irfan; Sorawee Porncharoenwase; Zvonimir Rakamaric; Neha Rungta; Emina Torlak
relevance_to_p1: Shows toolchain reliability and empirical study around Dafny itself.
core_claim: Random program generation can expose soundness and precision issues in the Dafny verifier and compilers.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to position P1 near empirical verification-engineering work on Dafny, while distinguishing proof brittleness from tool testing.

title: A Toolkit for Automated Testing of Dafny
authors: Aleksandr Fedchin; Tyler Dean; Jeffrey S. Foster; Eric Mercer; Zvonimir Rakamaric; Giles Reger; Neha Rungta; Robin Salkeld; Lucas Wagner; Cassidy Waldrip
relevance_to_p1: Shows that the Dafny ecosystem already supports testing-oriented auxiliary workflows.
core_claim: Automated unit testing and test generation can be layered onto Dafny programs and specifications.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as adjacent support tooling work: useful, but not focused on refactoring-induced proof breakage.

title: DafnyBench: A Benchmark for Formal Software Verification
authors: Chloe Loughridge; Qinyi Sun; Seth Ahrenbach; Federico Cassano; Chuyue Sun; Ying Sheng; Anish Mudide; Md Rakib Hossain Misu; Nada Amin; Max Tegmark
relevance_to_p1: Directly relevant benchmark work in Dafny, but aimed at LLM-era formal verification rather than maintenance under evolution.
core_claim: DafnyBench provides a large training/evaluation benchmark for ML-assisted formal software verification.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to show that Dafny benchmarking exists, while arguing that P1 targets a different phenomenon: refactoring-induced proof brittleness.

title: On the Impact of Formal Verification on Software Development
authors: Eric Mugnier; Yuanyuan Zhou; Ranjit Jhala; Michael Coblenz
venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages (OOPSLA)
relevance_to_p1: Strongly relevant empirical work on Dafny use in practice.
core_claim: Interviews with experienced Dafny users show that verification impacts software development in substantial but effortful ways.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to support the claim that effort and maintainability matter for adoption, which motivates P1.

title: Toward Automated, Contamination-free Dafny Benchmark Generation
authors: Changjie Wang; Mariano Scazzariello; Dejan Kostic; Marco Chiesa
relevance_to_p1: Relevant because it shows active work on building better Dafny benchmarks.
core_claim: TACoDafny proposes automated generation of Dafny benchmark tasks designed to reduce contamination and improve evaluation realism.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as very recent benchmark context, while noting that P1's benchmark dimension is maintenance/refactoring rather than task synthesis.

title: Local Success Does Not Compose: Benchmarking Large Language Models for Compositional Formal Verification
authors: Xu Xu; Xin Li; Xingwei Qu; Jie Fu; Binhang Yuan
relevance_to_p1: Relevant benchmark paper for compositional reasoning in Dafny.
core_claim: DafnyComp shows that local verification success does not transfer cleanly to compositional multi-function verification tasks.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to contrast P1's focus on evolution over time with newer LLM-era Dafny benchmark work.

title: Re:Form -- Reducing Human Priors in Scalable Formal Software Verification with RL in LLMs: A Preliminary Study on Dafny
authors: Chuanhao Yan; Fengdi Che; Xuhan Huang; Xu Xu; Xin Li; Yizhi Li; Xingwei Qu; Jingzhe Shi; Chenghua Lin; Yaodong Yang; Binhang Yuan; Hang Zhao; Yu Qiao; Bowen Zhou; Jie Fu
relevance_to_p1: Relevant to the LLM-era Dafny benchmark landscape and automated formal verification framing.
core_claim: RL and curated formal-language feedback can improve scalable Dafny verification tasks and introduce DafnyComp-like benchmark settings.
how_p1_uses_it: Use as adjacent LLM-era Dafny benchmarking, not as direct evidence about maintenance.

title: MutDafny: A Mutation-Based Approach to Assess Dafny Specifications
authors: Isabel Amaral; Alexandra Mendes; Jose Campos
relevance_to_p1: Relevant to specification quality and empirical analysis in Dafny.
core_claim: Mutation testing can expose weaknesses in Dafny specifications across real-world programs.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to show there is emerging work on empirical assessment of Dafny specifications, though not on refactoring-induced proof churn.

title: REFACTOR: Learning to Extract Theorems from Proofs
authors: Jinpeng Zhou; et al.
relevance_to_p1: Useful as a broader proof-refactoring/automation signal in theorem proving.
core_claim: Learning to extract reusable theorems from proofs can improve downstream proving performance.
how_p1_uses_it: Use only as distant adjacent work if the section expands beyond P1's current scope.
notes: Not cited in the compact section by default.

title: Towards AI-Assisted Synthesis of Verified Dafny Methods
authors: Md Rakib Hossain Misu; Cristina V. Lopes; Iris Ma; James Noble
relevance_to_p1: Directly relevant to LLM-based Dafny generation and baseline framing.
core_claim: Prompting and retrieval can substantially improve the synthesis of verified Dafny methods.
how_p1_uses_it: Use to show that recent Dafny work often targets generation/synthesis, whereas P1 targets maintenance after evolution.

title: Testing Specifications In Dafny
authors: Unknown from proceedings page
